entities.name: Luxuriosus

attributes.appearance: form of a charming maiden of twelve years

entities.raw_quote: one named Luxuriosus, another Superbus, and the third Avarus, [all of them] devils. And the first appeared to him in the form of a charming maiden of twelve years...

entities.name: Superbus
entities.raw_quote: likewise, during the night he adored that devil as a god... Likewise, he said that when he passed along the roads and was with the demons and found a cross, the devils fled from it...

entities.name: Avarus

attributes.function: associated with avarice (avaritia); key motive in necromantic divination

entities.raw_quote: ...the spirit of avarice (i avaritia ) is a key motive of the necromancer, and sometimes of his client...

entities.name: Johannes Cunalis
entities.raw_quote: this Johannes Cunalis had a book of necromancy [librum de nigromancia ], and that when he who spoke opened this book at once there appeared to him three demons...

entities.name: Jubertus

attributes.role: associate or master of Johannes Cunalis

entities.raw_quote: it is perhaps no distortion to suggest that the spirit of lust (luxuria) is one main incentive for the psychological experiments, the spirit of pride (superbia ) is a prime factor in the illusionist ones...

entities.name: CLM 849
entities.raw_quote: The link between necromancy and witchcraft emerges also from the work of Johannes Hartlieb... Clm 849 (e.g., the anointing of a boy’s fingernail for scrying, or the conjuring of a demon in the form of a horse for magical transportation)...

entities.name: Sancta Maria Heremitta
entities.raw_quote: ...one night with the aid of demons his master had a bridge constructed over a river in Bavaria at a place called Sancta Maria Heremitta.

entities.name: Johannes Fabri
entities.raw_quote: ...as he had done in the duchy of Austria in the case of a man called Johannes Fabri of Vienna.

entities.name: Johanneta
entities.raw_quote: ...he had proposed to blind Johanneta, the widow of Johannes Paganus of the present place...

entities.name: poison-making
entities.raw_quote: ...poisons were made by the aid of devils... taken from a basilisk, toad, serpent, spider or scorpion...

entities.name: herb-gathering

attributes.timing: feast of Saint John the Baptist

entities.raw_quote: ...on the feast of Saint John the Baptist he gathered certain herbs for medicine... on bended knees he first adored them, then extracting them in the name of his devils...

entities.name: keys (ritual use)
entities.raw_quote: With two keys he had traced her image, in a manner and form which he explained in the examination...

entities.name: Holy Friday

attributes.offering: three or five pence to Luxuriosus

entities.raw_quote: ...to Luxuriosus he gave three or five pence on Holy Friday before Easter...

entities.name: cross-desecration
entities.raw_quote: ...made a cross on the ground, and spat on it three times, and trampled on it three times with his left foot, and urinated and defecated on it...

entities.name: Table B

attributes.content: classification of necromantic types in Clm 849

entities.raw_quote: Table B . Types of necromancy found in Clm 849 — Illusionist / Psychological / Divinatory...

entities.name: Jubertus’s spirits correlation
entities.raw_quote: Correlation: Superbus — grandeur of display... Luxuriosus - seduction or rape... Avarus — recovery or discovery of goods...

summary: This chunk presents testimonial evidence from a trial involving Johannes Cunalis and his demon conjurations (Luxuriosus, Superbus, Avarus), describes ritual practices (cross-desecration, herb-gathering, poison-making), links these to the Munich Handbook CLM 849, and introduces Kieckhefer’s Table B classifying necromantic types. It also discusses manuscript compilation theory and possible authorship.
key_passages: one named Luxuriosus, another Superbus, and the third Avarus, [all of them] devils. And the first appeared to him in the form of a charming maiden of twelve years...

...the spirit of lust (luxuria) is one main incentive for the psychological experiments, the spirit of pride (superbia ) is a prime factor in the illusionist ones, and the spirit of avarice (i avaritia ) is a key motive...
